title: trisetum dregeanum
intro: Trisetum dregeanum, also known as Drege's oat-grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Trisetum dregeanum include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-arthritic agent. Herbal preparations of Trisetum dregeanum can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the plant to use as a dietary supplement.
